title: Romenagri / ILM — Reversible Multiscript Substrate for Systems Programming (Hindawi Programming System, 2004)
description: Romenagri (GPL 2003): reversible, ASCII-7, diacritic-free, C-identifier-legal transliteration kernel unifying Brahmi, Perso-Arabic, and NW-Semitic script families. First realised in the Hindawi Programming System (public release 15 August 2004): the first complete non-English systems-programming stack — C, C++, Assembly, Lex, Yacc, BASIC, JVM-target in Indic languages through an unmodified GCC toolchain. 68-language keyword registry (201 constructs), 45 Brahmi-derived script projection tables, four language bindings (C, JS, Java/Android, WASM). Measured reversibility: 98.68% reversible-or-canonical on exhaustive [a-z]^1..4 (2003-04 kernel). Copyright (C) 1993-2026 Abhishek Choudhary.

dates.description: Initial public release of Hindawi Programming System v1
